dads-lemon-icebox-pie.name: Dad's Lemon Icebox Pie
instructions: Preheat the oven to 163°C (165 degrees C).
Crush 28 wafer cookies with a food processor or by hand. Pat crushed cookies and melted margarine into the bottom of a 9-inch pie pan. Stand remaining whole wafer cookies around the edge of the pan.

Zest 1 tablespoon from lemons and set aside. Squeeze 1/2 cup fresh lemon juice. Combine lemon juice, lemon zest, sweetened condensed milk, and egg yolks in a mixing bowl. Mix well and pour into the prepared crust.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15 minutes. Remove from the oven, but leave the oven on.

Beat egg whites in a glass bowl on high speed, while slowly adding sugar, until almost stiff and peaks form. Arrange meringue on the pie with a spatula, spreading up to where it's touching the standing wafer cookies.
Bake in the preheated oven until browned, about 15 minutes, checking frequently. Let cool completely, 20 to 30 minutes. Chill in the refrigerator for at least 4 hours.

daikon-salad.name: Daikon Salad

ingredients.item: Toasted white and/or black sesame seeds

instructions: Toss daikon and ½ tsp. salt in a medium bowl; let sit 30 minutes. Transfer to a dish towel and squeeze out excess moisture.
Meanwhile, whisk chile, garlic, oil, vinegar, sugar, and remaining ¼ tsp. salt in a large bowl and let sit 30 minutes.
Add daikon to bowl and massage dressing into daikon. Let sit at least 30 minutes and up to 6 hours (cover and chill if holding longer than 1 hour).
To serve, stir in desired amount of chile crisp if using and top with sesame seeds.

dale-taldes-korean-fried-chicken-with-ki.name: Dale Talde’s Korean Fried Chicken With Kimchi Yogurt

ingredients.prep: such as nam pla or nuoc nam

ingredients.item: A spice mill or a mortar and pestle; a deep-fry thermometer

instructions: Toast cardamom pod, peppercorns, coriander seeds, and fennel seeds in a dry small skillet over medium heat until fragrant, about 2 minutes. Let cool.
Break open cardamom pod to remove seeds; discard pod. Finely grind cardamom seeds and the other toasted spices in spice mill.
Whisk hot sauce, yogurt, fish sauce, sesame oil, chicken bouillon, garlic powder, turmeric, chili powder, paprika, and toasted spice mixture in a medium bowl to blend.
Do ahead: Kimchi yogurt can be made 4 days ahead. Cover and chill.
Place chicken in a large bowl and add half of kimchi yogurt; turn to coat. Chill at least 4 hours and up to 4 days. Reserve remaining kimchi yogurt for serving.
Fit a large cast-iron skillet or Dutch oven with thermometer; pour in oil to a depth of 1” and heat over medium-high heat until thermometer registers 300°.

Whisk ¾ cup rice flour, 1 tsp. salt, and 1 cup water in a large bowl until completely smooth. Remove chicken from marinade, wiping off excess with your hands. Add to batter, turning to coat. Working in batches, carefully add chicken to skillet and fry, turning often to keep it from sticking, until golden brown and an instant-read thermometer inserted into thickest part of pieces registers 160° for breasts and wings and 165° for thighs, 10–12 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet and let cool.

Increase oil temperature to 350°. Whisk fish sauce, cayenne, remaining ¾ cup rice flour, and ¾ cup water in a large bowl until completely smooth. Working in batches, add fried chicken and turn to coat, then carefully add chicken to skillet and fry, turning often to prevent it from sticking, until golden brown and instant-read thermometer inserted into thickest part of pieces registers 160° for breasts and wings and 165° for thighs, 6–8 minutes. Serve chicken with reserved kimchi yogurt for dipping.
Do ahead: Chicken can be fried for the first time 1 day ahead. Cover and chill. Bring to room temperature before frying the second time.

date-marshmallow-waldorf-salad.name: Date-marshmallow Waldorf Salad
instructions: Place the apples in a medium serving bowl and sprinkle with sugar, lemon juice and salt. Stir in the celery, dates and walnuts. In a separate bowl, whip the cream with an electric mixer until it can hold a peak. Mix in mayonnaise and gently fold into the apple mixture along with the marshmallows. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving.

decadent-baked-mac-and-cheese.name: Decadent Baked Mac And Cheese
instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C).
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add penne and cook, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, about 11 minutes.

Meanwhile, melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Stir in cream, half-and-half, and garlic salt. Add Gruyere cheese, 1/2 cup mozzarella cheese, Parmesan cheese, and Oaxaca cheese. Stir into cream until melted. Drain pasta and add to the sauce; stir to coat.
Spray a casserole dish with cooking spray and pour in pasta mixture. Sprinkle with remaining 1/2 cup mozzarella cheese.
Bake in the preheated oven until cheese is bubbly and slightly browned, 15 to 20 minutes.

delicious-biscuits-and-light-condensed-m.name: Delicious Biscuits And Light Condensed Milk
delicious-biscuits-and-light-condensed-m.original_name: Delícia de bolacha e leite condensado light
instructions: Light the 500 ml of milk with the lemon peel and let it boil. Take it off the fire and let it cool off a little bit.

Throw the condensed milk and the yolks in a pot and stir. Add the masena flour, mix and add the milk without the lemon peel. Shake well and take it to the fire, stirring always until boiling. Mix the cold milk with vanilla.

In a deep container, have alternate layers of cookies, previously passed by vanilla milk, and cream (the last layer should be cream). Let it cool down and take it cold until it gets very fresh. When serving, sprinkle with ground biscuit and tastefully decorated.

delicious-tuna-bowl-comedera.name: Delicious Tuna Bowl

ingredients.item: Seeds of black ham, to taste

instructions: For vinegar, put into a bowl or glass to shredder the elote.
Add olive oil, cherry vinegar, fresh parsley and salt to suit.
Jumps well, remove with a spoon so that you can get them to shredded and mix everything.
It should result in a creamy dressing as you see.
Put in a bowl the Roman salad, the broken carrot.
Also add previously drained tuna, avocado, cherrys tomatoes.
Peel and cut the baked egg and add to bowl.
Fold the vinegar and decorate with black ham seeds.

dirty-south-hot-tamales-with-jackfruit-a.name: Dirty South Hot Tamales With Jackfruit And Cilantro Sauce

ingredients.item: . diamond crystal or  0.5 tsp.morton kosher salt

instructions: Drain jackfruit in a colander and rinse well. Transfer to a kitchen towel, gather ends, and squeeze tightly to remove as much liquid as possible. Sort through jackfruit for pieces with a fibrous core; cut away and discard cores. Pull jackfruit into small pieces with 2 forks. Return to kitchen towel; squeeze again to remove any remaining liquid. Set aside.

Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-low. Cook celery, bell pepper, and onion, stirring occasionally, until starting to soften, about 5 minutes. Stir in garlic, tomato paste, liquid aminos, Creole Seasoning, and ¼ tsp. salt. Cook, stirring constantly, until slightly darkened and fragrant, about 2 minutes. Stir in broth and reserved jackfruit. Simmer, stirring occasionally, until starting to thicken, 18–20 minutes. Taste filling and season with more salt if needed. Let cool.
Place corn husks in a large bowl and pour in warm water to cover. Weigh down with a plate to keep husks submerged. Let soak while you cook the grits.

Drain grits in a fine-mesh sieve. Bring broth to a boil in a medium saucepan; reduce heat to low. Whisk grits, salt, chili powder, paprika, and cayenne into broth. Simmer, whisking every few minutes to avoid clumping and scraping bottom of pan to prevent sticking, until liquid is absorbed and grits are tender, 15–20 minutes.
Scrape grits into a medium bowl, stir in oil and baking powder, and chill until cool, about 30 minutes. (Cooled grits should have a spreadable consistency similar to fresh ricotta.)

Spread out a husk on a work surface with the wide end farthest from you. Spread ½ cup grits on center of husk, leaving 2" at sides, 3" at bottom, and 1" at top. Place ¼ cup filling in center of grits and form into a log down middle of tamale. Fold long sides of husk together, gently pressing grits to seal in filling, then fold over to one side. Fold narrow end under tamale and transfer tamale to a large plate. Repeat process with remaining filling and grits.

Fit a stockpot with a metal steamer insert and pour in water to reach bottom of steamer basket. Bring water to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Place tamales upright in basket with open end facing up. Cover pot and steam tamales, checking every 20 minutes and adding more water if needed, 50 minutes.

Remove a tamale from pot and let cool 3 minutes. Remove husk; grits should be somewhat firm and hold their shape. If tamale is not done, carefully refold and return to pot. Cook tamales 5 minutes more and check again. Repeat as needed until tamales are done. Remove pot from heat, uncover, and let tamales rest 10 minutes.

While the tamales are resting, cook garlic, oil, and coriander in a small skillet over medium heat until fragrant, about 1 minute. Let cool, then transfer to a blender and add jalapeño, lemon juice, 1 cup cilantro, and ¼ cup water. Blend until smooth; season with salt.
Transfer tamales to a platter; open up. Drizzle with sauce; top with cilantro sprigs. Serve with pickled cauliflower.

does-a-bear.name: Does A Bear...?

ingredients.item: Zinfandel or other full-bodied red wine

instructions: Fill a cocktail shaker with ice. Add liqueur, vinegar, lemon juice, and simple syrup. Cover and shake vigorously until frost forms on outside of shaker. Strain into a highball glass filled with ice. Top with sparkling water, then add Zinfandel. Garnish with lemon wheel.

dont-passover-this-party-mix.name: Don’t Passover This Party Mix
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°. Line a large-rimmed baking sheet with a silicone baking mat or parchment paper. Toss matzo and nuts in a large bowl to evenly distribute.

Whisk egg whites in a medium bowl until frothy. Add brown sugar, granulated sugar, soy sauce, garam masala, salt, pepper, and cayenne and whisk until thick. Pour over nuts and matzo and fold to coat. Spread out on prepared baking sheet and bake, stirring occasionally, until caramelized, 35–45 minutes.
Remove from oven, drizzle butter over, and toss to coat. Let cool.
Do Ahead: Party mix can be made 5 days ahead. Store airtight at room temperature.

dreams-of-chicken-and-beer-teleculinaria.name: Dreams Of Chicken And Beer
instructions: Wipe the chicken's breasts and legs, reject skins and bones and cut them into small pieces.

Take a fryer to the fire with oil and let it warm up. In a bowl, beat the eggs, add the flour, beer, garlic powder and chopped parsley, season with salt and pepper and wrap, without knocking, until it is homogeneous.

Pass the chicken pieces in the previous mixture and take them to fry, few at a time, between 3 and 5 minutes or until they are golden. Remove them, let them drip on a sheet of kitchen paper and then serve them. At work, go with salad to taste.

dried-cherry-and-pecan-fougasse.name: Dried Cherry And Pecan Fougasse

ingredients.item: Egg beaten to blend with 1 teaspoon water

instructions: Pour 1/2 cup warm water into 2-cup measuring cup. Sprinkle yeast, then 1 teaspoon sugar over; stir to blend. Let stand until yeast dissolves and mixture bubbles and looks creamy, 5 to 7 minutes. Add remaining 1 cup warm water, 3 tablespoons oil, and honey to yeast mixture.

Combine flour and salt in bowl of heavy-duty mixer; stir to blend. Pour in yeast mixture. Attach dough hook and beat at medium-low speed until flour is evenly moistened but looks shaggy, occasionally stirring flour into center, about 3 minutes. Increase mixer speed to medium and beat until dough pulls away from sides of bowl and climbs hook, about 10 minutes (dough will be soft and sticky like batter).
Combine cherries, pecans, and orange peel in medium bowl; toss to blend. Add to dough and beat 1 minute. Using sturdy spatula, stir dough by hand to blend completely.

Lightly oil large bowl. Scrape dough into bowl. Brush top of dough lightly with oil. Brush sheet of plastic wrap with oil and cover bowl, oiled side down. Let dough rise in warm draft-free area until doubled in volume, 1 to 2 hours.
Gently turn dough over several times with spatula to deflate. Re-cover bowl with oiled plastic; chill overnight (dough will rise).

Sprinkle 2 large rimmed baking sheets lightly with flour or line with parchment paper. Using spatula, deflate dough by stirring or folding over several times. Divide dough into 2 equal pieces. Place 1 piece on lightly floured work surface; sprinkle top with flour. Roll out dough to 12x8- to 12x9-inch rectangle, sprinkling lightly with flour as needed to keep from sticking. Transfer dough to prepared sheet.

Using very sharp small knife, cut four 2-inch-long diagonal slashes to right of center of dough rectangle and 4 more to left of center to create pattern resembling leaf veins. Pull slashes apart with fingertips to make 3/4- to 1-inch-wide openings.
Repeat with remaining dough. Cover dough on each sheet with towel. Let rest 20 minutes.
Position 1 rack in top third and 1 rack in bottom third of oven; preheat to 232°C. Brush each fougasse with egg glaze; sprinkle with raw sugar and pierce all over with fork.

Bake fougasses 10 minutes. Reverse position of baking sheets, top to bottom, and turn sheets around. Bake fougasses until crusts are deep golden brown, about 8 minutes longer. Transfer fougasses to racks and cool 15 minutes. Serve warm or at room temperature.

dubai-gullac-recipe-lezzet.name: Dubai Gullaç Recip Home

ingredients.item: 3–4 mins of peanut paste (can be increased to)
Bitter can get a softer and sweet aroma using theikolata.
By increasing a part of the Kakaoine, you can catch a more dense & more balanced flavor.
Instead of cream, you can provide a denser and creamy consistency by adding half-package lab cheese.
You can get a more authentic taste using antep peanut cream instead of peanut paste.
You can mix the consistency with the taste of peanut paste and make it easier.
You can use roasted antep peanut or fill almond instead of roasted kadayif.
Rice instead of peanut, you can choose the Cognitive or Olives.
You can set a different taste balance by adding 1–2 tablespoons of honey instead of reducing the amount of sugar.
Make a lighter presentation by spreading coconut instead of cocoa sauce.
You can add fresh frela or frambuase in the presentation, add a strong fruit to the dessert.

duqqa.name: Duqqa
instructions: Preheat oven to 163°C (165 degrees C).
Spread 1 cup plus 2 tablespoons hazelnuts out on a baking sheet. Toast in the preheated oven until lightly browned and fragrant, about 10 minutes. Let cool.
Combine sesame seeds, cumin seeds, and coriander seeds in a pan over medium heat and toast until fragrant, 2 to 3 minutes. Remove from heat and let cool.
Combine toasted hazelnuts, toasted sesame seed mixture, dried mint, salt, and za'atar in the bowl of a food processor; pulse until evenly ground.
